notes: The technology base for mid-line storage; used in PCs for years; reliability risen enough for low duty-cycle enterprise use
Implicit primary product brand; ATA enterprise drives up to 300 GB per disk
High-performance disk interface; 142/72/36 GB per disk; FC/SCSI disks are the dominant enterprise tier
High-performance disk interface co-dominant with FC; subject of price decline but still much higher $/GB than ATA
Primary enterprise storage network for the survey respondents; mid-line storage must integrate into existing SAN/NAS environments
Secondary enterprise storage network; mid-line appliances must connect to existing NAS infrastructure
Framework for managing data movement across storage tiers as value and access patterns change over time
Data protection technology; mid-line appliances use RAID for reliability; eliminates tape drive and media errors
Emerging concept mentioned — quickly restores data to any point in time; mid-line disk enables this approach
Mentioned as ultra-performance tier for performance-intensive database applications above high-performance disk
